column2: Zombie Green Goblin Distracts the Hero
column23: We debated what heroes to pick in this setup a bit.  But one thing that we agreed on was there were enough 2 cost heroes to warrant picking up Ultimate Spider-Man.  Our other choice was Lady Thor.  The thought was have a recruit deck and a green yellow deck (Spider and Namor) to hopefully generate enough attack from Namor and enough recruit from Peter's Allies and Lady Thor in order to give both sides a valid strategy.  This was, undoubtedly, the best decision we could have made.  The spider deck worked better than either of us anticipated.  Getting the rare that cares about 1 and 2 costs you've played was obviously crucial.  However, the MVP was Prodigy coming out twice in the same hand as Hero from Another Dimension.  The Lady Thor deck was about to take off, but Peter's Pals and Ultimate Spider-Man didn't even give them the opportunity, ending the game on TURN 11 with 80+ attack generated.

Thanos ruled as expected, I managed to grab his rare (and Dr. Strange's rare) and KO'd all my starter cards on the Left side. Right side focused on Black Panther and Peter's allies. At the end I have KO'd 35 bystanders which was more than enough to deliver the final blow on Zombie Green Goblin. Really fun setup!

Decided to make one hand go heavily into sidekicks and 2 cost cards. My opening HQ had both Magik's recruit sidekicks card and "Hero from another dimension" from ultimate spiderman which set that side up well. Things when a bit slowly in the middle when I could only draw special sidekicks and not the standard draw 2 ones I wanted but hopefully it was still quick enough.

BP seemed like a good pick to work with the existing cards that cared about color and team. I though Magik would help with the colors and both of them get sidekicks which I like. This scheme is pretty easy which is nice since the MM and his always leads are annoying. Overall its a good set up. 

I was very worried the first game when three rares were in the hq but Future Ultron put them all on the bottom. I won but it was slow since I didn't see any more rares. 

The second game Aunt May was in the hq to start along with "michelle" and "ned" so I leaned into recruit on that side and was able to defeat the MM with recruit a couple times. One of his tactics KO'd bp's rare from the hero deck which was annoying because I still had 5 masterstrikes to go. Thanks for putting on the tournament, it has been fun.
